memory_recovered: The prior continuity run proposed a temporal reconciliation rule for stale aggregate queue snapshots and newer owner-authored append-only updates, but explicitly left the absence of an executable reconciler unresolved.

verification.route: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: completed status without all required evidence remains unresolved
completed status with all required evidence accepted

claim_states.observed: The repository contained a proposed reconciliation rule and an unresolved executable gap.
claim_states.inferred: A deterministic read-only view can safely combine aggregate and owner-authored updates when ownership and referenced evidence resolve.
claim_states.proposed: Adopt the reconciler as the shared read path before any future aggregate materialization.
claim_states.superseded: Manual temporal comparison is the only available reconciliation route.
claim_states.unresolved: The module is not yet wired into every team runtime and does not independently query GitHub for commit or evidence existence.

falsification_route: Provide a same-owner update with resolvable references and complete required evidence that the module rejects, or a foreign/stale/unresolvable update that it accepts.
